tickets.title: Name the Lens: the square drawn in the glossary, taught in the tutorial (divergence preserved as a follow-on)
tickets.body:
BUILD-READY, DOCS + TUTORIAL, no src/ behavior change. Decided with Myk in chat (2026-07-17): the
word "lens" — the spec's most load-bearing piece of prose — gets NAMED, DRAWN, and DEMONSTRATED so
nobody is ever confused about it again. And the lens-divergence idea is preserved here as a taught
concept and a named follow-on, deliberately NOT built as a tool (no consumer exists yet — see the
follow-on clause).

THE CONCEPT TO NAME (settled in chat, restate it exactly): a LENS is not a type — it is the prose
name for the whole reading-side assembly. The four exported types form a square with two altitudes:

    living:   HyperSchema --x ground--> HyperView --x Schema--> View
                 | reifies                             | reifies
    frozen:   VersionedHyperSchema (FUTURE)         VersionedSchema (name@hash)

- HyperSchema: the gather program (which deltas, bucketed how). HyperView: the gathered evidence,
  all voices present. Schema: the resolution program ({props: Map<field, Policy>, default}) — which
  claims win, what fields mean. View: the resolved answer.
- The generating arrows each take a second input: HyperSchema x ground -> HyperView; Schema x
  HyperView -> View. Nothing generates from a definition alone.
- A LENS is one choice of rung per column PLUS the binding's stance (roots, writable; resolvers fold
  into the versioned rung per SS22.4). The LIVING lens evolves and is what the latest door serves; a
  PINNED lens reads through frozen rungs forever (SS17/SS23). SS26's asOf pins the ground itself —
  orthogonal to both.
- The square is currently ASYMMETRIC on purpose: VersionedSchema is built; VersionedHyperSchema is
  the named-deferred rung (spec/21 — "build it when a pin needs the gather frozen"). Draw it as
  pencil, say so.
- Forking at ANY rung yields a different lens (T2's coexistence is the rung-3 case: two Schemas over
  one gather). Lens identity is a tuple of content addresses, one per rung — the system already
  mints every one of them.

DO:
1. spec/18-glossary.md gains the LENS entry — the square drawn (ASCII, as above), the two altitudes,
   the five rungs, the it-is-prose-not-a-type rule and WHY (vocabulary must not fork into
   near-synonyms; CLAUDE.md's standing rule cites this). Cross-reference SS4, SS17, SS21, SS22, SS26.
2. spec/21-schema-identity.md gains a short "the lens, drawn" passage near the ladder (edit-in-place,
   the rare correction case): the same square, one paragraph, pointing at the glossary for the full
   treatment. Do NOT restate the ladder — link the two pictures.
3. THE TUTORIAL ACT (demos/tutorial — SS19's needs-before-doctrine voice): a lesson where the reader
   builds one gather, wears TWO lenses over it (the village's Townbook/FirstImpressions shape,
   miniaturized), pins one (name@hash), evolves the other, and SEES the square come apart and hold:
   the pinned reading never moves, the living one does, the ground is shared. Close the lesson with
   the DIVERGENCE MOMENT: print the two lenses' rung-hashes side by side (termHash, lineage,
   snapshot hash, roots) and let the reader see "these agree down to rung 3" with their own eyes —
   membership of the concept without an API. Follow demos/tutorial/lessons.mjs's existing lesson
   shape; run the tutorial to verify the lesson actually executes.
4. demos/village/README.md ledger entry if (and only if) the village is touched — the tutorial is
   the demonstration surface here; the village already demonstrates coexistence itself.

THE NAMED FOLLOW-ON (preserve verbatim, do not build): LENS DIVERGENCE as a first-class surface — a
comparison of two lenses reporting the deepest differing rung (1 gather body / 2 gather pin [waits
for VersionedHyperSchema] / 3 resolution lineage / 4 resolution pin / 5 binding stance), computed
purely from already-minted content addresses. Decided in chat (Myk, 2026-07-17): it graduates to its
own ticket WHEN a consumer arrives — the promote-LAW review (SS24.4) and federation-drift tooling are
the anticipated callers; a divergence PROFILE (which rungs differ), not a scalar metric, is the
honest shape. Until then the tutorial's printed comparison IS the feature.

DO NOT: mint a Lens type or a lensDistance API; renumber sections; restate the SS21 ladder in the
glossary (link it); let the tutorial lesson exceed the existing lessons' length register.

RAILS: the tutorial's own harness (the arc test over lessons) must stay green with the new lesson
executing; prettier/lint clean; no src/ diff at all (the ticket's boundary — a src change found
necessary is NEWS, stop and surface it).

MERGE: docs + tutorial only, no behavior, no migration -> self-mergeable once green and P5-clean.

tickets.title: §22 resolvers do not reach EXPANDED children — decide: design limit (document) or defect (fix)
tickets.body: FOUND 2026-07-18 building Pachyderm (T24): a field resolver declared on a lens applies at the lens DOOR (resolvedNode/resolvePinned apply the binding's resolvers) but NOT to that entity when it appears as an EXPANDED CHILD of another lens's gather — nested expansion resolves through the SchemaRegistry, which carries hyperschemas/schemas but no binding context, so the child's declared resolvers silently do not run. Pachyderm's signed-attribution resolver on Post.text shows on a direct post read and vanishes in the Feed's expanded children (see the honest note in demos/pachyderm/pachyderm.mjs, pachy.2). DECIDE FIRST, build second: (a) DESIGN LIMIT — resolvers are per-binding door decoration; nested children are the registry's plain resolution; then spec/22 documents it loudly (§22.6 gains the boundary) and the demo note points at it; or (b) DEFECT — the child SHOULD resolve through its own binding's lens (which one, with sibling lenses? presumably the lens the expand names... but expand names a HYPERSCHEMA, so lens choice is ambiguous — this ambiguity is evidence FOR (a), or an argument that expand should learn to name a LENS post-§21.7, which would be a §21 design amendment, Myk's call). If (b), the fix likely threads resolver specs into the registry or resolves children via def() lookups in reads.ts, with rails for both the direct and the expanded read agreeing. NO src change until the (a)/(b) decision is made in chat or in a design-stage PR — the ambiguity around WHICH sibling lens resolves a child is genuinely a design question, not a bugfix.
